Charges: 5
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

Race: ALL

Useless?
QuoteReply
# May 09 2006 at 3:37 AM Rating: Default
Temperance
View User Forum
Scholar
514 posts
I try to click this item for an illusion of being a tree, but all I get is
You are unable to change form here.
The spell lands, rooting me for 36mins (I can click it off) but the
illusion won't stick in most places I try.

Is this thing good for anything other than being a memento of spending too
much time in the casino? (I buy these from the merchants there.)
____________________________
~ Muse Temperance the Elf
~ Lyricist of <Mystic Coercion> on Erollisi Marr
~ with a <Variety> of alts to keep me busy

Illusionists Stone

# Feb 24 2006 at 5:10 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786252033
15 posts
This is NOT a hard thing to get. I get it off the Magician looking guy. ALL THE TIME. One note to take is I always bet 4 tokens. I have gotten the Snakefingers (AWSOME thing to have)off the ranger guy. Great for pulling. From what I have found betting 4 seems to be best bet to get the good stuff.

if you have used this before what do you look like?

Confusion
QuoteReply
# Mar 16 2004 at 12:53 PM Rating: Decent
Nihixul
View User Forum
Scholar
261 posts
I would imagine that this confusion started around mistaking this spell effect with that of , which does add regen.

That said, this is a fun little item. As a rogue who collects clicky items and illusion items in particular, this was a wonderful little prize I never thought I would be able to find (pre-casino). :)

Good luck to all the gamblers!
____________________________
Urnihixul Noctumbra

~Order of Seibwen/Ancient Fellowship/In Via Damnum/Alliance of Hope~
108 Rogue
▲ Page top
RE: Confusion
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2006 at 11:52 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591863751171
877 posts
Well, Spirit of Oak (the spell you linked above), would be in the line of the one confusion would've resulted from. Druids have a Treeform spell, which is likely what this spell may be confused with. Treeform does offer regeneration; it's the Druid tree-illusion spell. "Illusion: Tree", on the other hand, is the Enchanter's version.
▲ Page top
10K
QuoteReply
# Feb 27 2004 at 2:11 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591801678987
344 posts
This goes for about 10K, in the Bazaar on Povar. I'd get it just for the regeneration effect, if it wasn't so outrageously priced, or actually had some other use once the charges were depleted.

# Apr 01 2004 at 9:16 AM Rating: Decent

151 posts
If they said it could be recharged in neriak then they are confusing this with the froglock illusion stone that is recieved in Neriak and is recharged there.
▲ Page top
Just my 2cp
QuoteReply
# Dec 24 2003 at 12:05 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361749446
9 posts
I'm one of those warriors that can never have enough clickies. Its really sad when you get to the point that you can fill all 15 of your buff slots by yourself and still have more clickies that you haven't used yet. Anyway, this is one of those obscure treasures that nobody seems to have ever heard of. Those that will take the time to look it up will probably think nothing more of this than a toy with no practical use. For the most part that's right... being a tree doesn't do much for you anymore... Being a tree used to make you indifferent to almost anything but undead but in many zones it doesn't seem to make much of a differnce anymore.
One of the benefits of this item that most people overlook is the self root effect. I like to think of this effect as a 36 minute self root that also makes you look like a tree. What benefit could that possibly have you ask? Consider mobs that fear... when a mob fears you, you are going to run away from it uncontrollably. Spirit of Wolf / Eagle complicates things even more since you will run farther away before the fear wears off. When you are feared its easy to run off and get aggro and possibly even wipe your raid. If you are standing in front of a mob in treeform it can fear you all it likes and you will not move an inch... when fear wears off you just turn back around and continue fighting. I remember back in my younger years when we used to kill Wuoshi they would tell everyone to turn RUN off and click off your sow because of the fear. This item solves that for you, especially if you are doing ranged damgage and having the mob move a bit doesn't affect you much. This same scenario would also apply to mobs that charm... if you are charmed you will not be able to melee attack your group because you are rooted. The nice part about the root effect is that you can click it off whenever you choose and the effect is INSTANT. Now in order for this item to be practial you really need to get two or three of them and keep them recharged. Granted, this item will not work indoors and its application is somewhat limited but some of us like to prepare for anything, just my 2cp.
Just found 2 of these for sale on my server, fun fun

Edited, Wed Dec 24 00:12:18 2003
▲ Page top
